seoTitle: How to Know if Your Shopify Store Is Really Live 2026
metaDescription: Not sure if customers can actually see your Shopify store? Here's how to confirm your store is published, your domain works, and checkout is live.
intro: New Shopify sellers often finish setup and still aren't sure whether anyone else can actually see their store. This guide covers the quick checks that confirm your store is live: store status, domain connection, product visibility, and whether checkout actually processes a payment. Running through these steps means you find gaps before a customer does.

steps.heading: Check your store status
steps.body: Start in your Shopify admin and confirm the store is set to published rather than left on a password-protected or draft state.

steps.heading: Test your domain connection
steps.body: Open your custom domain in a browser — ideally from a different device or network — to verify it points to your live store rather than a placeholder page.

steps.heading: Verify product visibility
steps.body: Check that the products you want customers to see are marked as active and available on the online store sales channel, not hidden or set to draft.

steps.heading: Confirm checkout and payments work
steps.body: Run a test purchase or use Shopify's test payment options to make sure customers can actually complete checkout, not just browse.

faq.question: Why does my Shopify store show a password page to visitors?
faq.answer: Shopify stores start with password protection enabled by default during setup, so if you haven't turned it off in your online store preferences, visitors will hit that page instead of your storefront.

faq.question: How do I know if my domain is properly connected?
faq.answer: Open your custom domain in a browser on a device or network you haven't used to edit the store, and confirm it loads your actual storefront rather than an error or Shopify placeholder page.

faq.question: Can I check if my store is live without making a real purchase?
faq.answer: Yes — Shopify offers a test payment mode you can enable to run through checkout without charging a real card.

faq.question: My products aren't showing up on the storefront — why?
faq.answer: This usually means the product's status is set to draft or it hasn't been added to the online store sales channel, both of which you can fix from the product page in your admin.
